name: Moya v. United States
summary: Federal appellate decision addressing procedural due process requirements in criminal proceedings and evidence presentation standards.
holding: Due process requires adequate opportunity to challenge evidence and confront witnesses; procedural safeguards must be meaningful, not merely formal.
constitutional_basis: Fifth Amendment Due Process, Sixth Amendment Confrontation Clause
relevance_to_audit: Addresses evidentiary adequacy and confrontation rights; relevant to administrative proceedings requiring testimony and documentary evidence.
